post.description:
Accept a file, normalize it to SVG, extract layers, and create a job.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 for invalid options or input the converter rejects;
        415 if the file's MIME type has no registered converter.

Add a file to the library, deduplicating by content hash.

If a file with the same SHA-256 already exists, the existing record is
returned and ``existing`` is ``true`` — no second copy is stored.
Exception: when the request carries conversion ``options`` (e.g. the
operator changed the bitmap algorithm or palette in SourceSection and
clicked Apply), and those options differ from what was used last time,
we re-process the file in place — overwriting ``normalized.svg`` and
``meta.json`` — instead of silently returning the stale conversion.
Without this branch the operator's option edits would never reach the
plotter, because the editor re-uploads the same bytes to apply changes.

get.description: List library entries. Filters/sort applied server-side.
Return the distinct folder names currently used by library entries.

schema.title: Response List Folders Files Folders Get

get.description: Return one library entry, including its SVG and layer metadata.

patch.description: Rename a library entry and/or move it to another folder.

delete.description: Remove a library entry and its on-disk artifacts.

schema.title: Response Delete File Files  File Id  Delete

get.description: Stream the original uploaded bytes back to the client.

List the raster algorithms the bitmap converter can apply.

Returns:
    One entry per registered algorithm, for the UI to offer as choices.

List the single-stroke Hershey fonts available for text rendering.

Returns:
    Sorted font names for the typography panel to offer as choices.
List the configured machine profiles, sorted by name.

post.description:
Create or update a profile from a JSON body and persist it.

The profile is written to the writable user directory, overriding any
bundled profile with the same name. FastAPI returns 422 if the body fails
validation.

get.description:
Return a single profile by name.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no profile with the name exists.

delete.description:
Delete a user profile by name.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no profile with the name exists; 400 if the
        profile is bundled (read-only) and therefore cannot be deleted.

schema.title: Response Delete One Profiles  Name  Delete

get.description:
Export a profile as YAML.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no profile with the name exists.

post.description:
Validate and persist an imported YAML profile.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 422 if the YAML is malformed or fails validation.

Optimize toolpaths to reduce pen-up travel.

Args:
    request: The SVG to optimize plus optional per-layer settings.

Returns:
    An :class:`OptimizeResponse` with the optimized SVG, refreshed layer
    descriptors, and before/after travel metrics.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if the SVG cannot be processed.

Generate G-code for a print plan.

Returns the program plus the resolved-plan snapshot (and its hash)
that produced it — the snapshot is also persisted to SQLite for
later inspection via ``GET /plans/{hash}``.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if the profile is unknown; 400 if the plan
        fails business validation; 422 if generation itself fails.

Validate and estimate a placed drawing before generating it.

The response is a :class:`PreflightReport` with ``plan_hash`` set so
the frontend can verify it later matches the ``/generate`` hash.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if the profile is unknown; 400 if the SVG
        cannot be parsed or the plan fails business validation.

get.description:
Return the resolved plan archived under ``plan_hash``.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no snapshot exists for that hash.
Return the current connection and streaming status.

post.description:
Open a serial connection to the plotter.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if the serial port cannot be opened.
Disconnect from the plotter.
Jog the head by a relative offset.
Move the head to an absolute workspace position.
Home the machine.
Start streaming a G-code job.
Pause the running job.
Resume a paused job.
Abort the running job.

get.description:
List print runs, active ones first.

Protected because ``PrintRun.pause_points`` carries operator-facing
prompts (e.g. "Insert pen slot 1: Red") that reveal the pen carousel
configuration when an API key is configured.

post.description:
Enqueue a G-code program for the plotter.

Supplying an ``Idempotency-Key`` header makes retries safe: if a run already
exists with that key, it is returned instead of creating a duplicate.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if the profile is unknown.

get.description:
Return a single print run.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if the run is unknown.

delete.description:
Delete a run from the queue.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if unknown; 409 if it is currently streaming.

schema.title: Response Remove Queue  Run Id  Delete

post.description: Pause a run that is currently streaming.
Resume a paused run, continuing it or re-queuing from its checkpoint.
Cancel a run, aborting it if it is currently streaming.

get.description: List recent sensitive actions, newest first.
List recent processed jobs, newest first.

Return a single job record.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no job with the id exists.
List the available raster conversion presets.
List the saved macros, sorted by name.

post.description: Create or update a macro from a JSON body and persist it.

delete.description:
Delete a macro by name.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no macro with the name exists.

schema.title: Response Delete One Macros  Name  Delete

post.description:
Execute a macro's commands on the connected plotter.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if the macro is unknown; 409 if not connected or a
        job is running.

schema.title: Response Run One Macros  Name  Run Post

post.description:
Run a quick bitmap vectorisation and return the SVG.

Args:
    file: Uploaded image (PNG / JPEG / TIFF / WebP / HEIC).
    algorithm: ``direct`` / ``halftone`` / ``stippling``.
    options: Optional JSON object mirroring ``BitmapOptions`` (the same
        keys ``/upload`` accepts).
    quality: ``draft`` / ``standard`` / ``final``. Trades latency for
        fidelity — see ``_QUALITY_TIERS`` for the concrete knobs.

Returns:
    A :class:`PreviewResponse` with a fast-rendered SVG and the palette.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 415 if the MIME is not a supported raster image;
        413 if the upload exceeds ``MAX_PREVIEW_BYTES``;
        400 if options can't be parsed or the quality tier is unknown.

Render a typography draft to single-stroke Hershey SVG.

Args:
    file: Uploaded ``.txt`` or ``.md`` source. Truncated to
        ``MAX_PREVIEW_TEXT_BYTES`` so a runaway paste never pins the
        event loop; the response flags whether truncation happened so
        the UI can warn.
    options: Optional JSON object mirroring
        :class:`~pen_plotter.typography.TypographyOptions`.

Returns:
    A :class:`TypographyPreviewResponse` carrying the SVG ready for
    the EditPreviewPane.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 415 if the MIME isn't text/markdown; 400 if the
        options can't be parsed or the font name is unknown; 413 if
        the upload is empty.

Re-render the cached bitmap job with optional per-layer algorithm overrides.

Args:
    request: The job id and per-layer algorithm choices.

Returns:
    The freshly rendered SVG (sanitized) plus rendering warnings.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if no segmentation cache exists for the job
        (e.g. after a backend restart or LRU eviction); the UI then
        re-uploads instead.

get.description: Report the package version and (if available) the git checkout state.

Fetch from the remote and report whether a newer commit exists.

This is the polling endpoint behind the optional "update available"
startup toast; it never mutates the working tree (only ``git fetch``)
and tolerates offline machines by returning ``update_available=False``
with an ``error`` string instead of raising.

post.description:
Run the bundled ``update.sh`` script and return its log.

The script pulls the latest commit on the current branch and rebuilds
the frontend. If a new commit is applied, the backend needs a restart
to load the new Python code — we report that in the response so the UI
can prompt the operator.

Pass ``{"force": true}`` to discard uncommitted local changes before
pulling. Use sparingly — this is irreversible.

Extract text and image blocks for every page of a PDF.

Args:
    file: A PDF upload; other MIME types are rejected with 415.

Returns:
    A :class:`DocumentAnalysis` listing the blocks of each page.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 415 when the upload is not a PDF; 413 if the
        file exceeds the analysis size limit; 422 if PyMuPDF cannot
        parse the document.

get.description:
Report that the API is reachable and which version is running.

Returns:
    A payload with a fixed ``ok`` status and the package version.

AlgorithmInfo.title: AlgorithmInfo
AlgorithmInfo.description: Metadata describing one raster algorithm choice.

Block.title: Block
Block.description:
One detected region on a PDF page.

Attributes:
    id: Stable identifier ``p<page>-<t|i><index>`` — see module docstring.
    kind: ``"text"`` or ``"image"``.
    bbox: ``[x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max]`` in millimetres, top-left origin.
    text_sample: First ~60 characters of the block's text (text blocks only).
    char_count: Number of non-whitespace characters (text blocks only).

BoundingBox.title: BoundingBox
BoundingBox.description: Axis-aligned bounding box of a layer's geometry, in millimeters.

CheckUpdateResponse.title: CheckUpdateResponse
CheckUpdateResponse.description: Result of ``GET /system/check-update``: is a newer commit available?

steps_per_mm.title: Steps Per Mm
steps_per_mm.description: Motor steps per millimeter of Cartesian travel.

servo_up.title: Servo Up
servo_up.description: Servo pulse width for the pen-up position, in 83.3 ns units (EBB SP).

servo_down.title: Servo Down
servo_down.description: Servo pulse width for the pen-down position, in 83.3 ns units (EBB SP).

servo_rate.title: Servo Rate
servo_rate.description: Servo travel rate between up/down, in EBB SC units.

serial_terminator.title: Serial Terminator
serial_terminator.description: Line terminator the board expects; EiBotBoard uses CR.

EbbConfig.title: EbbConfig
EbbConfig.description:
EiBotBoard (AxiDraw-class) motion parameters.

Required only for profiles whose ``gcode_dialect`` is ``"ebb"``. The two
motors form an H-bot, so a Cartesian move maps to mixed motor steps.

EnqueueRequest.title: EnqueueRequest
EnqueueRequest.description: Body for adding a run to the print queue.

FileDetail.title: FileDetail
FileDetail.description: Library entry plus its converted SVG and per-file metadata.

FilePatch.title: FilePatch
FilePatch.description: In-place edit of a library entry — rename and/or move between folders.

FileRecordOut.title: FileRecordOut
FileRecordOut.description: Public projection of a :class:`FileRecord`, ready for JSON.

FileUploadResponse.title: FileUploadResponse
FileUploadResponse.description: Result of uploading to the library; ``existing`` flags dedup hits.

GenerateRequest.title: GenerateRequest
GenerateRequest.description:
Wire model for ``POST /generate``.

Identical to :class:`PrintPlan` plus an explicit override flag.
Kept as a named subclass so the OpenAPI schema (and therefore the
generated TypeScript types) reads ``GenerateRequest`` at the
endpoint boundary, while the rest of the backend manipulates the
domain type.

GenerateResponse.title: GenerateResponse
GenerateResponse.description: Generated G-code, its line count, and the resolved plan snapshot.

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse
HealthResponse.description: Response body for the health check endpoint.

ImportRequest.title: ImportRequest
ImportRequest.description: A profile to import, as a YAML document.

Job.title: Job
Job.description: Tracks a single file through the conversion-to-plot lifecycle.

JobRecord.title: JobRecord
JobRecord.description: A persisted summary of one processed job.

LayerAlgorithm.title: LayerAlgorithm
LayerAlgorithm.description:
A per-layer algorithm override applied on top of the cached defaults.

Backwards compatible: callers can still pass a single ``algorithm`` /
``algorithm_options`` pair (the legacy shape). When ``passes`` is set
and non-empty, it overrides the single-algorithm fields and the layer
is rendered as the stack of passes in order.

LayerInfo.title: LayerInfo
LayerInfo.description: A single separated drawing layer and its plotting parameters.

LayerOptimization.title: LayerOptimization
LayerOptimization.description: Per-layer optimization settings supplied by the client.

LayerPass.title: LayerPass
LayerPass.description:
One rendering pass within a multi-pass layer override.

A single colour can be drawn with several stacked algorithms — e.g.
``contours`` for the outline followed by ``crosshatch`` for the fill
— so the operator gets the visual effect of multiple inks while
keeping the layer on one physical pen.

LayerPlan.title: LayerPlan
LayerPlan.description:
Per-layer print settings — the unified pivot type.

Replaces the old ``GenerateLayer`` Pydantic model and
``LayerGeneration`` dataclass. Every field is optional except
``layer_id``; defaults are filled in later by
:func:`pen_plotter.application.plan_resolver.resolve_plan` using
the active machine profile.

Note on ``optimize`` / ``simplify_tolerance_mm``: today the SVG
inside the surrounding :class:`PrintPlan` is already optimized by
the frontend's ``optimize → preflight → generate`` pipeline, so
these fields are traceability data — they ride along into the
resolved plan and the persisted snapshot, and they participate in
``plan_hash`` so equal SVG + different optimize intent cannot
collide. A later refactor (lot L3+) will let the application
services drive optimization directly from these fields.

MachineProfile.title: MachineProfile
MachineProfile.description:
Describes a target pen plotter.

All machine-specific behavior lives in profile files rather than code, so a
new plotter can be supported by writing one of these (typically loaded from
YAML) without touching the application.

Macro.title: Macro
Macro.description: A user-defined sequence of raw plotter commands triggerable as one action.

OptimizeResponse.title: OptimizeResponse
OptimizeResponse.description: Optimized SVG, re-extracted layers, and travel metrics.

PageBlocks.title: PageBlocks
PageBlocks.description: All detected blocks on one PDF page.

PaletteEntry.title: PaletteEntry
PaletteEntry.description: One cluster colour and its share of the image (0..1).

PenSlot.title: PenSlot
PenSlot.description: A physical pen position in the machine's magazine.

PlacementPlan.title: PlacementPlan
PlacementPlan.description:
Sheet placement inside the machine workspace.

Mirrors :class:`pen_plotter.models.Placement` — kept as a separate
domain model so the pivot is self-contained and doesn't pull legacy
types into the new package boundary.

PlanMetadata.title: PlanMetadata
PlanMetadata.description:
Provenance information attached to a plan.

The client may set ``client_version`` so a stored snapshot can later
be correlated with the UI release that produced it.

Point.title: Point
Point.description: A point in the machine's coordinate space, in profile units.

PreflightReport.title: PreflightReport
PreflightReport.description: Pre-run safety and estimation checks for a placed drawing.

PreflightRequest.title: PreflightRequest
PreflightRequest.description: Wire model for ``POST /preflight`` — same shape as a print plan.

Preset.title: Preset
Preset.description: A named bundle of converter options.

PrintPlan.title: PrintPlan
PrintPlan.description:
What the operator wants drawn, before profile defaults apply.

The same instance flows through ``/preflight`` and ``/generate`` so
both endpoints provably operate on identical inputs.

PrintRun.title: PrintRun
PrintRun.description: A persisted print job in the production queue.

RerenderResponse.title: RerenderResponse
RerenderResponse.description: The freshly rendered SVG plus any non-fatal warnings.

ResolvedLayer.title: ResolvedLayer
ResolvedLayer.description:
A layer with every setting resolved against the active profile.

No ``None`` survives here: ``drawing_speed_mm_s`` falls back to the
profile speed, ``simplify_tolerance_mm`` falls back to the engine
default (0.05), and ``pause_before`` keeps the operator's choice
(``"auto"`` / ``"always"`` / ``"never"``) for the engine to act on.

ResolvedPlan.title: ResolvedPlan
ResolvedPlan.description:
The plan after defaults + validation, ready to feed the engines.

Carries the original ``PrintPlan`` plus the per-layer resolution and
a deterministic content hash. Engines never re-read the raw plan;
they read the resolved one. Snapshots stored in SQLite are exactly
instances of this model.

ToolpathMetrics.title: ToolpathMetrics
ToolpathMetrics.description: Pen-up travel before and after optimization, in SVG user units.

TypographyPreviewResponse.title: TypographyPreviewResponse
TypographyPreviewResponse.description: Hershey-rendered SVG plus the truncation flag the UI surfaces.

UpdateResponse.title: UpdateResponse
UpdateResponse.description: Result of a ``./update.sh`` invocation, suitable for the UI to render.

UploadResponse.title: UploadResponse
UploadResponse.description:
Result of an upload.

Carries the created job, the normalized SVG pivot, any non-fatal
warnings, and converter-specific metadata (e.g. ``page_count`` and the
selected ``page`` for multi-page PDF / DOCX / HTML inputs).

VersionResponse.title: VersionResponse
VersionResponse.description: Currently-installed OmniPlot version and git state.

WorkspaceBounds.title: WorkspaceBounds
WorkspaceBounds.description: Rectangular drawable area of a machine, expressed in profile units.
